title: The Rainbow Fruit World and the Great Lemon Fish Debate
summary: Seeker acts as a peacekeeper, first resolving a dispute between Dimitar and a sick mouse named Hattie over a single delicious fish. After suggesting they split it, Seeker is summoned by Rainbow and Pink Mergirl, who are fighting over their stuffed animals. To help them calm down, Seeker encourages them to 'stomp and squeak' before sewing a new seal stuffy so both can sleep peacefully. Their day concludes with a magical journey to Rainbow Fruit World using a tiny rainbow potion. There, they meet a Grape Cat and explore a land of blueberry oceans and pear sharks. Dimitar successfully catches a lemon fish, splashing everyone with juice, before they use a final potion to return to the castle.

highlightQuote.text: Stomp and bounce and squeak out loud!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: You want to climb back up or slide back up?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: All right, there's the stars. You want to be on my lap?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: You lap lap lap.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Your lappy lap.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Dad you sat on your eyes!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh! Your things are on something. What am I sitting on?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Your bunny's neck!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, on his neck? I'm so sorry, bunny. Oh no, the stars fell down.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And daddy, I thought you could move it to my bed head.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: All right, let me fix the stars. Like that?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah. And this be ready.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay, where are you going to be?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: In your lap.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay, get in. And you're sitting... whoa whoa whoa. Okay. There we go. Ding-dong at the Seeker's gate! Who is it at the Seeker's gate?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It's Dimitar.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, hello Dimitar. Come on in. Now Dimitar, I heard that maybe you were having an argument with someone.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Who was it?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Were you having a little argument with Hattie?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Or what?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Or maybe it was with one of the Mergirls.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Um... did you remember? I think we need to ask um... I need to call Hattie on my phone 'cause she's in bed because she's sick.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh. Hattie! And Hattie answered, "Ahem, yes, this is Hattie."

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Um... did you have an argument with me yesterday?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Yeah, we did have a little bit of an argument, unfortunately.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: About what?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well, there was just this one delicious fish. And we both really wanted to eat it. And we couldn't agree on who would get it because you said, "Well, you always like fish, so you should have the fish," and I said, "Well, I'm learning about fish and I wanted to try it." And so we got into a little bit of an argument.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And then there's a ring-a-ring-a-ring on Seeker's phone.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh! Someone's calling Seeker. Yes, hello, who is it?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It's the... it's Rainbow Mergirl. I'm having a playdate with with Pink Mergirl. So um we're having a big argument about which stuffy we're going to take to bed.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, that can be very difficult to decide, Seeker. So what did you do about that? Did you get really mad?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, we just had a big argument and want you to come and help us solve it.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, okay. Well first of all, Seeker, I have to solve this argument between Dimitar and Hattie. But then I'll come over, okay? I'll be right over.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: We'll just keep arguing.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay, well, while you wait, try to stay calm and be kind to each other because remember that you love each other. Okay, bye, I'll see you soon. And so Seeker said, "Well Hattie, listen. I think that you could come up with a solution about the fish with Dimitar between the two of you."

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Oh... and then ding-a-ring-a-ring.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, who is it?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It's Rainbow Mergirl. Oh yes... um... I need Hattie just... I need Hattie.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well, Hattie's feeling sick and she's in the middle of an argument with Dimitar, but what do you need her for?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Well, well, but I need Dimitar, I need him.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh okay. And Dimitar says, "Ah, here I am, yes."

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Um... you can cut the fish in half. So the head is going to be for Hattie.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And the tail and the body is going to be for you.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, that sounds wonderful! Let me tell Hattie. Hey Hattie, we could just cut it in two! And Hattie said, "Okay, I get the head." And Dimitar said, "Well, oh, okay, the head is delicious but you can have the head and I'll have the tail." And Hattie said, "Great!" And so then they were both happy and they resolved their argument. And then Seeker said, "Well, now that we've figured that out, I've got to go talk to some Mergirls." And so Seeker went to visit Rainbow Mergirl and Pink Mergirl and he said, "Okay, what seems to be the problem, Mergirls?"

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: We're having a fight about which stuffy we're going to take to bed.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay. So Rainbow Mergirl, which stuffy do you want to take to bed?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Well I want to take to bed her bunny, but she said no because she wants to take my lion stuffy. And we got into an argument about which one of those stuffies.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, that sounds so hard. And and so what do you think you can do about it, Seeker?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Don't know, 'cause we don't have any solutions. We're out of them. Yep.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: That's horrible. And and how are you feeling, Rainbow Mergirl? Are you feeling mad?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Mad!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh. Well, sometimes the first step is just to get your mad out. What can you do to get your mad out, Seeker?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Stomp and bounce!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Good idea.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And squeak out loud!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, squeak out loud, that's a good one. Okay. And so the Mergirls both stomped and bounced and squeaked. And then did they feel a little calmer after that?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yep.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay. And now Seeker said, "Let's just talk about what everyone needs. Rainbow Mergirl, what do you need for bedtime to be calm and cozy?"

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I have no stuffy except that one bunny stuffy.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, and you need the bunny stuffy to go to sleep.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yep. And and I need a second one because Rainbow Mergirl has two, a lion and a seal. says Pink Mergirl.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh. And and...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: She she and she wanted to get a new star-shaped one. Yeah. So that was our plan until she thought not to, and then that's when we got into the... that's when we got into the argument.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh. Why did you decide not to have the star-shaped stuffy?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: 'Cause I really liked the seal.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: The seal. Oh, okay. It was cozy when I felt it, said Pink Mergirl.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And oh! Our plan is that I could both... I could sew one for Pink Mergirl.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh! What could you sew for her?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: A seal stuffy!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh! Sew sew sew sew sew... wow, that was fast. Okay. Sew sew sew sew sew.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Pink Mergirl, now what do you think about your seal stuffy?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh. So now does that mean they both have a seal stuffy?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Whoo!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Rainbow Mergirl said, "Okay, I have a seal stuffy," and Pink Mergirl said, "I have a new seal stuffy too!" And so now we both have what we need.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Right. Now don't touch my feet 'cause I like them to be cold.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, okay. And so Pink Mergirl and Rainbow Mergirl were both very happy that they had their own seal stuffies, and it's a good thing too because it was just in time for bedtime. And Rainbow Mergirl curled up with her seal stuffy and Pink Mergirl curled up with her seal stuffy and everyone was happy.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: But then...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And then the Dimitar said, "Potion!"

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: "Potion?" said Seeker. "You want to go somewhere? Did you eat your fish already?"

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yep.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay. Well, um... how about we pick a potion, but then we'll have to tell the story of what happens in our next Stars and Stories? What do you think?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, no, we can...

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: You want to do... you want to do a quick potion?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: A quick potion.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay. Maybe we could... Seeker, maybe we could um pick one of these very small potions. Normally we have these ones that are the size of like a drinking glass, but I have really little ones that are just like the size of your fingernail. These teeny tiny potion bottles. In fact, I've never tried any of these but they're very interesting colors. Do you want to pick one out? What color do you want?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: A pink and purple, silver and gold, and red. Or the rainbow one.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, yeah, there's one that's got all the colors of the rainbow, Seeker. Well, I'll take off its teeny tiny cork... plop! and give it a sniff. Hmm, it doesn't really smell like anything. You have a better nose than I do, Dimitar. Do you smell anything?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I take it... mwah.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: What does it smell like?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It smells like pear and lemon.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Pear and lemon, wow. I think this is going to be an interesting one. All right, let's both take a sip, sip, sip, sip. And then Dimitar said the rhyme.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Ding-dong cadong-dong, ding-dong cadong-dong, ding-dong cadong-dong.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And then there was a zwoop! And they zwooped into Rainbow Fruit World. And everything was just made of fruit.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And then a fruit cat came out.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: What flavor was the fruit cat? Was it like a grape cat or a lemon cat?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: A grape cat.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: It was a cat made of grapes. And the grape cat said, "Meow meow, welcome to Rainbow Fruit World! What are you doing here?"

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Seeker said, "Well we're just... we're just visiting. Could you show us around?" And Dimitar said, "Yeah, do you have anything salty?" And and grape cat said...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Like fish? Like fish?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And grape cat said, "Wow! Do you have any grape fish? Grape fish?"

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: "You know, it's funny," said Grape Cat. "I wouldn't have told you we don't have anything salty, but now that you mention it, we do have some fish. They're not made of grapes though. They're actually made of um... of lemon. Lemon fish."

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Oh.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: "So we have to go over to the um... the ocean, which is made out of... of pomegranate seeds. A huge ocean full of pomegranate seeds with the lemon fish swimming in it. Shall we go over there?"

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay. And they walked...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Oh! Also with pear sharks.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Whoa, Pear Sharks! I love that. Ooh, what an interesting pomegranate seed ocean. And they walked through the forest where the trees were made of bananas and... what other kind of fruit?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: A... and apples.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And apples. There were apple trees and banana trees, but they weren't trees with apples and bananas on them, they were just... the whole tree was made of giant apples and bananas. It was a very crazy place. And so they walked through the apple-banana forest and they made it to the beach where the sand was made of blueberries and there were waves crashing on the shore made of pomegranate seeds.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, no, no! Oh, we could change the ocean to be a blueberry ocean with a pomegranate seed beach.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, I love that idea. And so it changed in the blink of an eye. And now the pomegranate seeds were the beach and the ocean was made of blueberries and there were waves crashing onto the beach made of blueberries and it smelled amazingly blueberry-y, and also a little bit pomegranat-y. And out in the water, they spotted a pomegranate... no, a Pear Shark swimming and also a Lemon Fish.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And um Seeker said to Dimitar, "Do you know how to swim?" And Dimitar said, "Of course I do!" And he flew up, and as he was flying up he said... he turned back to the grape cat and said, "Excuse me, grape cat, can I eat that lemon fish?" What did the grape cat say?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Meow meow meow meow!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, what did that mean?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It means yes.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Dimitar just said, "Hehehe," and he flew up high and then dove straight down and with a splash of blueberry shooting everywhere, covering everyone with blueberry juice, he dove down and then swooped back up again with...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And Dimitar, you... you covered everyone with blueberries!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: "Yeah, I'm all covered with blueberry juice too, but I've got this delicious lemon fish. Mmm, mmm, mmm, so lemony. It's not salty but I still like it."

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And what did everyone say?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Seeker said, "Wow, this Rainbow Fruit World is amazing!"

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: But Seeker got blueberry on him too.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Only a little bit on his boots. "Oh oops," said Seeker, "I got a little bit on my beautiful red cloak. I'm going to wipe it off. Flick, flick, flick. All better." And Grape Cat said, "Yeah, fruit juice is a bit of a hazard here for people that come with clothes, but people that are made of fruit don't mind, like me." And Seeker said, "Well, I think that's about all the time we have for our Fruit World adventure. We better get going home because this was just a tiny potion bottle, so we could only have a tiny adventure." And Seeker and Dimitar drank the going home potion and... zwoop! found themselves back in Seeker's castle. And that's the end of Stars and Stories.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Shadow puppet!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay.

characters.name: Seeker
characters.description: A brave dwarven explorer and mediator, he resides in a glowing castle hub—a multiversal dock for skyships anchored within the skyline of New York City. From this urban sanctum, he conducts silver slime experiments and tends to wandering cats amidst a massive library. Frequently swapping between a red coat and a mystical purple cloak, his braided red beard remains his most eccentric tool, capable of catching small fish. A master of fusion, he combines ancient stone-craft with high-velocity mechanics, utilizing a legendary magic ladder to scale the shifting architecture of the realms. Whether brewing portal potions, harvesting floof-berries in Limbo with gemstone goggles, or aiding Buddy the Mouse, this walnut-loving student of the stars remains the hero who orchestrated the daring rescue of the world.

characters.name: Dimitar
characters.description: A sharp-witted resident of a circular island castle, this two-century-old purple dragonet carries a pirate’s salt-crusted legacy and a fierce devotion to his salvaged library. Formerly of the Cadasori galleries, Seeker’s grumpy companion now roams the deep to save his ancestral shores, though a recent volcanic disturbance has left him uncharacteristically sickened and frail. Driven by habit and a tireless appetite for fish, he bickers incessantly over snacks while guarding his remaining volumes with a brooding, protective intensity.

characters.name: Hattie
characters.description: A clever mouse milliner and engineer of whimsy, she is a resourceful spirit who crafts exquisite hats and soulful portraits within her hollow tree stump workshop. Though a formidable presence famously counted among the thirty legendary cats, she is currently confined to her bed by illness, her usual treks through saturated woodlands for rare tape and lollipops temporarily halted. Even in her malaise, her sharp mind lingers on a recent, stinging disagreement with Dimitar over a fish, a rare shadow cast upon a heart otherwise known for its boundless generosity and creative light.

characters.name: Rainbow Mergirl
characters.description: A resourceful mergirl known for her clever use of kelp to aid the Lanternshark, she balances her practical ingenuity with a spirited streak, recently exemplified by a sharp disagreement with the Pink Mergirl over their cherished stuffed animals.

characters.name: Pink Mergirl
characters.description: A compassionate spirit of the deep, she serves as a beacon for sharks denied a natural glow, illuminating their path through the abyss before retreating to her grotto to find cozy comfort in the embrace of a plush seal stuffy.

characters.name: Grape Cat
characters.description: A friendly resident of Rainbow Fruit World entirely made of grapes.

characters.name: Pear Sharks
characters.description: Creatures that swim in the oceans of Rainbow Fruit World.

characters.name: Lemon Fish
characters.description: Fish made of lemon that swim in the blueberry ocean.

places.name: Seeker's Castle
places.description: Behind an unassuming facade and a solitary doorbell lies Seeker’s home, a sprawling magical fortress that serves as a vital sanctuary for weary legends. The interior expands into a labyrinthine refuge where an expansive library of mystical books towers beside a potion closet that acts as the true gateway to adventure. This fortress is a hub for calls to action and well-earned rest, where Wobbly Whiskers waits to greet the Seeker and Buddy the Mouse—though the warmth of their homecoming is inevitably punctuated by his latest sticky mess upon the rug.

places.name: Hattie's Bed
places.description: Where the sick mouse Hattie rests while resolving her dispute.

places.name: Rainbow Fruit World
places.description: A magical dimension where every element of nature is composed of different fruits.

places.name: Blueberry Ocean
places.description: A body of water in Rainbow Fruit World made entirely of blueberries and juice.

places.name: Pomegranate Seed Beach
places.description: A beach where the 'sand' consists of pomegranate seeds.

places.name: Apple-Banana Forest
places.description: A woods where the trees are giant bananas and apples.
